title: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
text: goal project show C routine parallelized OpenMP can compiled inside R package exposed R thin Rcpp wrapper. three moving parts: parallel C routine , Makevars files turn OpenMP portable way, Rcpp function hands R’s data C routine. example routine walks numeric vector multiplies every element two, distributing loop iterations across available OpenMP threads.
code: . ├── DESCRIPTION                         # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                           # Function and dependency registration ├── R                                   # R functions │   ├── RcppCandOpenMP-package.R        # Package documentation │   └── RcppExports.R                   # Autogenerated R to C++ bindings by Rcpp ├── README.md ├── man                                 # Package documentation │   ├── multiply_by_two.Rd │   └── RcppCandOpenMP-package.Rd └── src                                 # Compiled code     ├── Makevars                        # Enable OpenMP on Unix-alikes (macOS/Linux)     ├── Makevars.win                    # Enable OpenMP on Windows     ├── my_c_parallel_func.c            # The OpenMP-parallelized C routine     ├── surface_c_fun_with_rcpp.cpp     # Rcpp wrapper that calls the C routine     └── RcppExports.cpp                 # Autogenerated R bindings

previous_headings: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
title: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
text: C routine lives src/my_c_parallel_func.c. <omp.h> header included compiler advertises OpenMP support _OPENMP macro, keeps routine compiling cleanly toolchains lack OpenMP. #pragma omp parallel directive splits loop across threads; firstprivate(x, n) gives thread copy pointer iteration count. n number elements x process: upper bound loop, n iterations OpenMP divides among threads. number threads governed OpenMP runtime (example, OMP_NUM_THREADS environment variable), argument.
code: #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h>  #ifdef _OPENMP #include <omp.h> #endif  void parallel_function(double *x, int n) {      int i;   #pragma omp parallel for firstprivate(x, n)   for (i = 0; i < n; i++){ x[i] *= 2; } }

previous_headings: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
title: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
text: OpenMP enabled src/Makevars (Unix-alikes) src/Makevars.win (Windows). Rather hard-coding compiler flags -fopenmp, files use R’s $(SHLIB_OPENMP_*FLAGS) variables, expand correct OpenMP flag whichever compiler R built . parallel #pragma lives .c file, OpenMP flag reach C compiler – PKG_CFLAGS = $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CFLAGS) ensures. Setting PKG_CXXFLAGS (C++ flag) leave my_c_parallel_func.c compiled without OpenMP, #pragma ignored loop run serially. PKG_LIBS links OpenMP runtime; using $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CXXFLAGS) appropriate shared object linked C++ driver. Using variables – rather literal flags – portable, CRAN-friendly way request OpenMP.

previous_headings: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
title: Using OpenMP with a C Routine and Rcpp
text: bridge R lives src/surface_c_fun_with_rcpp.cpp. parallel_function() compiled C, declared inside extern "C" block C++ compiler resolves C linkage. exported multiply_by_two() function passes raw double* buffer underlying Rcpp::NumericVector – obtained x.begin() – straight C routine, together element count n. // [[Rcpp::export]] attribute generates R-level binding. exported function also carries //' roxygen comments (trimmed excerpt ); Rcpp::compileAttributes() copies R/RcppExports.R, package’s R help page multiply_by_two() generated . R routine called :
code: #include <Rcpp.h>  // Ensure _C++_ can access the function. extern "C" {   void parallel_function(double *x, int n); }  // [[Rcpp::export]] void multiply_by_two(Rcpp::NumericVector x, int n) {    parallel_function( x.begin(), n ); } # Data x = 1:5  # Process all elements of x n = length(x)  # Call the routine multiply_by_two(x, n = n)
